assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Extended channel provides 24-month support vs 14 months for Regular channel in the comparison table and throughout the document

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Extra cost during extended period only (months 15-24)' in the table and states 'Additional cost for extended support' in the cost impact section

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response states 'Version arrival: Same as Regular (no delay)' in the table and explains that Extended keeps versions supported for longer periods

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: While the response mentions the risk of 'falling behind' and discusses operational considerations, it does not explicitly warn about missing new features or accumulating technical debt

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response states 'Good news: Extended channel is available for 1.31+ versions, so you can migrate directly' and includes command to check current status before migration

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Better for compliance requirements needing longer-term stability' and discusses how it suits organizations with strong change management requirements

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'End-of-extended-support enforcement — GKE will force upgrade when extended support expires' and emphasizes the 24-month limit

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response prominently highlights this as the 'Critical difference: Minor upgrades are NOT automated' and dedicates significant content to explaining the manual upgrade responsibility
